content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 22,
1994
Jan Wasson
3449 Beethoven St.
Los Angeles,
Ca 90066
Re:
Your Request for
Confirmation of Telephone
Advice
Our File No.
1-94-357
Dear Ms. Wasson:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the
‘Act”) 1
Since your request is one for
general information,
we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) 2
Your letter dated November 4,
1994,
correctly summarizes my
advice.
If there was any understanding with a vendor that Tony
Miller For Secretary of State 1994 would be allowed time beyond
the normal
30 day grace period to pay, an invoice,
the goods or
services received from the vendor would be considered an in-kind
loan.
If a loan aggregating $1,000 or more from a single source
is received within the late contribution/late independent
expenditure reporting period, it is necessary to file a late
contribution report.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et ,q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Sections
18329 (c) (3) .)
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Wasson,
Jan
No.
I
94-357
Page
2
If you have additional questions, please contact
me at
(916)
322-5662.
1
Wayne P.
Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.conclusion: If there was any understanding with a vendor that Tony Miller For Secretary of State 1994 would be allowed time beyond the normal 30 day grace period to pay an invoice, the goods or services received from the vendor would be considered an in-kind loan. If a loan aggregating $1,000 or more from a single source is received within the late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting period, it is necessary to file a late contribution report.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether in-kind loans and late contribution reporting requirements apply when a campaign vendor allows extended payment terms beyond the standard 30-day period.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Extended payment terms beyond the standard 30-day period constitute an in-kind loan. If such a loan aggregates $1,000 or more from a single source during the late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting period, a late contribution report must be filed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal written advice letter confirming prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers present, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable in the body text. Requestor's letter dated November 4, 1994 correctly summarizes the advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether in-kind loans and late contribution reporting requirements apply when a campaign vendor allows extended payment terms beyond the standard 30-day period.

CONCLUSION: If there was any understanding with a vendor that Tony Miller For Secretary of State 1994 would be allowed time beyond the normal 30 day grace period to pay an invoice, the goods or services received from the vendor would be considered an in-kind loan. If a loan aggregating $1,000 or more from a single source is received within the late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting period, it is necessary to file a late contribution report.

If there was any understanding with a vendor that Tony Miller For Secretary of State 1994 would be allowed time beyond the normal 30 day grace period to pay, an invoice, the goods or services received from the vendor would be considered an in-kind loan. If a loan aggregating $1,000 or more from a single source is received within the late contribution/late independent expenditure reporting period, it is necessary to file a late contribution report. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et ,q. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329 (c) (3) .) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Wasson, Jan No. I 94-357 Page 2 If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. 1 Wayne P. Imberi Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure provisions, clarifying that extended payment terms from vendors constitute in-kind loans and may trigger late contribution reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act.
